diagnosis: The level is a massive horizontal corridor with dense, repetitive enemy-and-platform patterns (types 1, 2, 3) and almost no vertical traversal — the player moves left-to-right at roughly the same elevation for 2.5 minutes. Two falls occurred at x≈2022 and x≈4857, both in mid-level sections where pit recovery was possible but the repetitive spacing made timing errors likely. The player completed 8 enemy kills and took 6 hits, confirming combat engagement, but rated the level only 3/5 and did not finish, explicitly citing lack of variance and repeated obstacles.

lessons.lesson: Horizontal length alone cannot substitute for structural variety — when a level extends for hundreds of tiles without meaningful changes in elevation, pacing, or encounter type, players perceive it as a grind rather than a progression.
conditions: no significant elevation changes over long stretches
lessons.outcome: player disengagement, mid-level falls from fatigue, incomplete run, mediocre rating despite functional mechanics
